BEAT APP
The ultimate narrative beat sheet

EOY 2026 · Out of Time

Story timeline · working draft
PRE-GAME
Treasure Party
PRE-GAME
PRE-GAME
PRE-GAME
1. Two ships in the cove
click to expand
Our ship + One Piece pirate ship docked side by side. Players goof around — cannons, rope swings, ship-to-ship chaos. Pure pirate fantasy while waiting.
Reference
2. Slone goes silent
click to expand
Hope alerts players: Slone has gone radio silent. Sets up the lobby task.
Reference
3. Boost the signal
click to expand
Players climb to the crow's nest and rig an antenna to restore comms with Slone. Purposeful lobby activity.
Reference
4. Treasure hunting fun
click to expand
Players know the treasure is somewhere in the cove, underwater. Jones leads the search — basically magnet fishing. They haul up goofy junk: a shopping cart, a rusty Chug Jug, a busted Crown Royale. Funny, low-stakes chaos to pass the time before the event kicks off.
Reference
ACT 1
Ripping the Veil
ACT 1
ACT 1
ACT 1
ACT 1
ACT 1
ACT 1
ACT 1
ACT 1
1. Treasure extracted
click to expand
EVENT STARTS. Finally the treasure is found, jones (or whoever we want) brings it up from the cove floor, its a big moment, we've been waiting all season for it!
Reference
2. Celebration
click to expand
the team opens the chest slowly, inside on a pile of gold, gems, potions and Vbucks, we see a new type of sprite, a TIME SPRITE! (we'll discover hes a time sprite soon enough). its sleeping snuggled up with THE TIME SLIVER! OMG theyre adorable :) we did it!  We found it the treasure!! Happy moment. Brief exhale before everything goes wrong.
Reference
3. Slone + Foundation arrive
click to expand
suddenly, coming through the anime veil, Foundation flies in carrying Slone ala Superman/Loise Lane landing on deck. foundation speaks: "Geno knows we've been hiding. He's coming." Slone spots the Sliver: "wow..you found the treasure?! theres a sliver of hope." 😬
Reference
4. Sprite blocks Slone
click to expand
Slone reaches for the Sliver. The Sprite, drawn to the Sliver from the start, and feeling protective of his precioussss...hisses violently. Won't let her near it.
Reference
5. Hope + Sprite connect
click to expand
Hope steps forward: "Let me try." Beckons slowly. Sprite hesitates… sniffs her hand… then melts — nuzzles her, scurries up her like a ferret. they're Bonded.
Reference
6. Slone: "Let's go!"
click to expand
Slone rallies everyone: "Let's get moving — we need to heal the ZP and save the island!" Hopeful beat. Then a voice booms from behind the players…
Reference
7. Geno's voice
click to expand
"NOT MUCH LEFT TO SAVE..IF YOU ASK ME." Voice booms from behind. Players turn, Geno's massive shadow form tears through the veil from outside!
Reference
8. Veil disintegrates
click to expand
A blast force radiates from Geno's tear — the veil and sky are eaten away from the breach outward, disintegrating in waves. Everything anime goes with it: the One Piece ship, the crew, all our miniseason allies — rifted back to their universe.  "NO — we're losing our friends!"
Reference
9. Luffy's impossible jump
click to expand
[ALT — if Luffy secured] As his ship vanishes, Luffy launches himself across the impossible gap — limbs stretching, crew screaming behind him — lands on our deck just before they're gone. One last friend.
Reference
10. The dead island revealed
click to expand
Behind Geno we see the scale of the HORROR! he real Fortnite island. Hollowed out, like an industrial wasteland. At its center — Geno's machine, ZP at its core, portal open above it. Through the portal: Geno's upside-down utopia, lush and impossible, assembled in real time from the precious resources he's bee leeching, island debris floating up through the rift. 
Reference
11. Hope + Jones react
click to expand
Hope (quietly): "I've seen this. I just hoped I was wrong."
Jones: "GENO — what have you done?!"
Geno: "What every great leader does. Sacrifices for his VISION. I took what was rightfully mine. And that includes… the Sliver."
Reference
12. Geno reconstitutes
click to expand
Geno pulls himself from shadow form back to human scale — floats down and lands on the deck of our ship, now beached on a dried-out dead cove. He came for the Sliver. Our heroes and players prepare to fight.
Reference
13. A hero dies — for real
click to expand
a potential moment to kill a named hero falls — [TBD: Lost Sister / member of the Order]. No respawn. Geno grins: "See, the great thing about controlling the ZP is that the loop is paused. You all wanted freedom — now you have it. But the cost of freedom is steep." Players feel it. Someone is gone.
Reference
14. Sliver football
click to expand
All-out battle. Geno lunges for the Sliver. Players toss it between each other — keep-away, chaotic, funny. Geno is furious. He gets close, closer… and grabs it. "Finally."
Reference
15. Sprite bites Geno
click to expand
The Time Sprite launches at Geno's hand — CHOMP. Geno howls in pain: "You disgusting little thing!!" In that split second, the Sprite snatches the Sliver — and GULPS IT DOWN. Swallowed.
Reference
16. Pause — what just happened
click to expand
Everything stops. Everyone — players, heroes, Geno — stares at the Sprite. The Sliver is gone. Inside a small feral animal. "...did that just happen?" The Sprite burps.
Reference
17. Geno grows
click to expand
Geno loses patience. He grows — bigger, bigger, massive — rising into the air. "Time to end it. Time to take the thing you love most — your HOME. I'll get the Sliver from your cold dead hands!"
Reference
18. The island shatters
click to expand
Geno comes down with a single earth-shattering blow — radial shockwave detonates outward. Players are thrown but land fast, pulled down by gravity. Heavy debris — chunks of island, buildings, cars — launches high and starts coming back down. A full semi truck tumbles through the air. Peely is inside it.
Reference
19. Impending doom
click to expand
Everyone on the ground, disoriented. Geno still on one knee, fist buried through the island. Debris raining down. A burning Fortnite Chopper tumbles directly at us — seconds from impact. Nowhere to go.
Reference
20. "STOOOOOOOP IT"
click to expand
A tiny high-pitched scream. Everything freezes. The Time Sprite — arms outstretched, legs planted, superhero pose — has stopped the burning Chopper dead in the air. One foot from its face. The Chopper is frozen. Time is frozen. The Sprite's eyes are wide. Everyone stares. What the hell just happened.
Reference
21. Out of Time moment
click to expand
Players can move freely. Everything else — crawling. Shimmering rift-butterfly time energy drifts through the air. The Chopper flames barely flicker. Debris hangs mid-fall. Trees suspended. And in the background, colossal Geno — fist still through the island, frozen solid. The world is at a fraction of our speed. We are the only thing alive in it.
Reference
ACT 2
Escape Fortnite
ACT 2
ACT 2
ACT 2
ACT 2
ACT 2
1. Understanding Out of Time
click to expand
Hope grabs the Sprite: "WHOOHOOOO!! You saved us!!" She throws him up like a baby — the moment he leaves her arms, everything rewinds. WHAT?! Jones, elated: "Does this mean what I think it means? Can we unwind this disaster??" Hope: "The Sprite is holding it all together. I don't know how long he can make this last."
Reference
2. The Plan
click to expand
Players look up — the ZP, still locked in Geno's machine, has been ripped free of the island and now floats high above Geno, portal still open, Geno's utopia visible through it. Debris still rising up through the rift. Slone takes charge: "NEW PLAN. The island is destroyed — but we still save the ZP. We need to reunite it with the Sliver, free it from Geno's control. It rebuilt the island before. It's our only hope."
Reference
3. Foundation takes the lead
click to expand
Foundation jumps in: "I know how we get up there — follow me!" Rushes toward the frozen Chopper. "EVERYONE GET IN. We'll reverse time and ride this burning hunk of metal up to the ZP." No discussion. Pure Foundation. Turns to Hope: "You've bonded with our sprite buddy — how much time we got?" Hope: "I don't know. But not a lot. He can't hold time forever. We have to hurry."
Reference
4. Elevator ride
click to expand
Everyone piles in, open doors on both sides. Time reverses — up we go. Burning debris rushes back up past us. The Chopper is a wreck at the bottom but slowly heals itself around players as we rise — fire dying, hull resealing, windows uncracking. The catch: chunks of debris that originally punched THROUGH the Chopper on the way down now retrace their path in reverse — threading back through the cabin the other way. Players watch the hull: a hole seals on one side = something is about to exit through the other. Shoot it before it hits you. Get hit and you're dead — no respawn. High action, high stakes, pure chaos.
Reference
5. Time is ending
click to expand
The Sprite strains — time pulses flicker, the world outside stutters. Hope feels it: "WE HAVE TO JUMP OUT — NOW! He can't hold it!" The Chopper has brought us part of the way up but not all the way. A debris platform hangs just within reach.
Reference
6. Foundation's end
click to expand
As time reverses, the engine un-explodes — the doorway clears. One by one everyone jumps to the debris platform. Foundation is at the door, getting everyone out. Hope is last — he flings her through the opening. Jones catches her: "I got you." They both look back. Time lurches forward. The engine re-explodes. Foundation is still standing there. He used the last clear second to throw her out instead of himself. The re-exploded engine engulfs him. He's gone.
Reference
7. "This was unexpected"
click to expand
In the shock and grief, Hope looks up at Jones. "You saved me." Jones: "I thought you could see the future?" Hope: "...this was unexpected." She blushes. Brief, human, clear — right there in the rubble. Jones looks up at the ZP machine. "Let's finish this."
Reference
8. The ascent
click to expand
Classic platforming upward through the debris field — mantling, jumping, double jumping, grappling hook. As the Sprite recovers, time dilation comes back online — players can now freeze or rewind debris mid-climb: pull a distant platform closer, flip a chunk to create a stepping stone, reverse a falling piece to ride it back up. The mechanic shifts from survival tool to puzzle tool. [expand later]
Reference
9. Where is Geno?
click to expand
Slone glances back at the background — Geno's colossal frozen form is gone. Just empty sky. "Where is Geno?! I don't like this!" Jones: "We can't stop, we gotta keep going." Slone: "Everyone — keep your eyes peeled."
Reference
10. Luffy's zipline
click to expand
[ALT — if Luffy secured] Luffy spots the Battle Bus crashing back down. He stretches his arms and grabs hold of it — players zipline across on his stretched arms toward the Bus. Luffy grins, about to swing himself over and join them—
Reference
11. Geno takes Luffy
click to expand
A rift tears open next to Luffy. Geno — human scale now, terrifyingly fast — smacks him clean out of our reality. Gone. Players watch from the Bus, helpless. Geno looks up at them. He knows exactly where we are. He's been watching the whole time.
Reference
12. He's catching up
click to expand
Every time players use time dilation from here, Geno gets closer. He's learning the pattern, closing the gap faster each time. Slone: "He's adapting. Don't use it unless we have to." The climb becomes a race — use the power sparingly or he catches us.
Reference
ACT 3
Battle on the machine
ACT 3
ACT 3
ACT 3
1. Climb the machine
click to expand
Players + Jones scale Geno's machine using time mechanic. Platform traversal under pressure. DV guards the ascent.
Reference
2. He's waiting for us
click to expand
Players reach the ZP machine. Geno is already there. Arms crossed. Calm. He figured out the game. He beat us here. " i see you took your TIME, I wondered when you'd arrive. " Act 3 begins.
Reference
3. Final battle
click to expand
Players use Time Sprite-enabled powers to fight Geno. Time-twist mechanic returns, now amplified.
Reference
4. Ascend to new world
click to expand
Players rise to Geno's new world. Sprites are critical to shaping it.
Reference
5. Sacrifice
click to expand
Someone stays behind so the others can escape (Hope? TBD). Geno trapped — in time loop or old world.
Reference
EPILOGUE
New beginning
EPILOGUE
EPILOGUE
1. Titan footprint
click to expand
Players land and look around — they're standing inside a massive footprint. CH8 theme revealed: The Titans.
Reference
2. Jones + bus driver
click to expand
Small, human moment. Jones and the bus driver. Torch passed. Jones carries his losses into CH8.
Reference
Click any beat to tag a note.

Or add one below.
Select a beat to add references
Click the 📎 on any beat
to manage its references.
JonesThe Optimist
Caring, impulsive, daring — the ultimate Fortnite player. Throws caution to the wind for his friends. Not a genius but a true hero. Hides centuries of loss (immortal, family died of old age) behind a joking, unserious mask. Has hidden info from Hope about the Zero Point. Survives into CH8.
FoundationThe Muscle · dies Act 2
Born leader, no BS, all action. Massively powerful. Stern and driven — but has his own agenda (came for Geno, not out of pure loyalty). Can be derailed by rage. Voiced by The Rock. Gets everyone out of the Chopper in Act 2 — stays behind, trapped by unshot debris when time snaps back. Engine explosion. No speech. Gone.
SloneThe Brains
Smart, sharp, all business. Former IO villain — near-death changed her, she chose the right side. ZP expert. Excellent tactician who prepares for every scenario. Stays behind during anime season as spy. Arrives with Foundation at event start. Her redemption makes her competence feel earned.
HopeThe Heart
All instinct and empathy. Canonically the personified spirit of Hope from Pandora's Box. Her graffiti murals ARE her visions of the future — established lore. Recently her visions went dark ("artist's block"), making her line "I've painted this. I just hoped I was wrong" land harder. Bonded with the Sprite. Sacrifice.
GenoThe Villain
Was hidden among the Seven. Draining the island to build his utopia. Genuinely believes he's right — "sacrifices for the dream." Adapts to time during the event — starts frozen, gradually learns to enter our time freezes to hunt allies. Trapped at end.
Dark VoyagerGeno's Lieutenant
Geno's enforcer. Guards the machine in Act 3. Final stand before players reach Geno. Defeated.
The SpriteComic Relief + Cosmic Pivot
Always a Time Sprite — was drawn to the Sliver from the start. Feral, Stitch-energy, protective. Bonds with Hope (she's the only one it trusts). Swallows Sliver in Act 3 → 100x power. Living time controller: flip = rewind, throw up = fast forward. Screams "STOOOOOP IT" and freezes a burning chopper.
Luffy (One Piece)ALT — TBD if secured
Like Jones — daring, stubborn, cheerful, confident. Natural parallel makes the collab feel narratively earned. In lobby on the One Piece ship. Makes impossible jump to our deck as his crew is rifted away. Role in event beyond that TBD.
✓ Sliver logic
Heroes want to complete ZP to heal the island. Geno fears a whole ZP — too powerful. Opposing goals locked. Twist TBD: does healing the ZP actually heal the island?
✓ Slone's role
Stays behind during anime season spying on Geno, sabotaging machines. Goes silent in lobby → crow's nest antenna task. Arrives with Foundation at event start.
✓ Hope + Sprite bond
Hope's time visions (murals) create a connection the Sprite recognizes. They bond at event start. She sacrifices knowing it's coming. The Sprite is what she loses.
✓ Sprite / Time Sprite
Always a Time Sprite. Swallowing the Sliver in Act 3 = 100x. Flip = rewind. Throw up = fast forward. Comic relief + cosmic pivot.
✓ Geno + time
Not immune — adapts. Starts frozen, gradually learns to enter our time freezes to hunt allies. Our power becomes our vulnerability.
⚠ Anime world / veil
The veil = Geno's distraction construct. When torn: blast force radiates outward, veil and sky disintegrate in waves — not burning paper, more like erasure. One Piece crew rifted back. ALL Act 1 beats are GAMEPLAY — players in control throughout. ALT: Luffy makes impossible jump to our ship before vanishing.
⚠ Jones ending
Survives but needs a defined wound into CH8. Bus driver moment should define his loss.
? ZP healing twist
Does completing ZP heal the island? Option A: too far gone, no — new world only. Option B: triggers catastrophe, validates Geno's fear.
? One Piece role in event
Luffy/crew present in lobby. Do they carry into the event or end at veil tear?
? Slone during the event
Arrives with Foundation. Does she fight, get separated, or have her own Act 1–3 beat?
? Who dies first in Act 1?
Needs to be someone players bonded with in the miniseason. Leading candidates: Lost Sister, a member of the Order. Decision affects emotional weight of the entire event.

4 / 44
Treasure Party
4. Treasure hunting fun
Gameplay
Players know the treasure is somewhere in the cove, underwater. Jones leads the search — basically magnet fishing. They haul up goofy junk: a shopping cart, a rusty Chug Jug, a busted Crown Royale. Funny, low-stakes chaos to pass the time before the event kicks off.
